item.title: Images of Note: Green Ubuntu
item.description:
Every single person who said they supported the "free Market" I have met have been the most authoritarian people who loved corporations and plutocracy.
I have returned. Fear me.
Green Ubuntu is also wrong, she's thinking of Ubuntu Mate.
If you make locked down versions of Linux, Linus Torvalds hunts you down with skills he learnt during national service and kills you.
I fucking hate this pic almost has much as I hate Super dry monster.
Install doom on the mechanism and get a Nobel Prize.
Of all the many concerning things I have heard trump say, this is the most concerning. Go fucking vote.
every Ad IBM made for Linux has been ominous. Cases in point: https://youtu.be/x7ozaFbqg00 https://youtu.be/fJA9eiUktcA
That is a smart move.
Girl's might.
me irl
O-O
We need more people dressing like JoJo characters.
Oh no
Bun's just vibin.
:O
Hey! You finally made it! http://discord.abbii.gay: For Chats http://twitch.abbii.gay: For Stream (m/w/f around 1830 UK time) http://tumblr.abbii.gay: For shitposts http://mastodon.abbii.gay: For microblogging
item.dc:creator: abbiistabbii

item.title: A pic of Flava Flav ⏰ and the US Women's Water Polo Team he sponsored.
item.description: Flava Flav has personally sponsored the US Women's Water Polo Team after finding out members of the team were quietly working multiple jobs to pay for training and competing even after winning 3 Olympic gold medals. He's calling other celebrities to do the same with other teams.
item.dc:creator: TequilaSnorter

item.title: Almost 2,000 years in age,the Roman aqueduct located in Zaghouan, Tunisia, stands as a remarkable example of ancient engineering. Spanning 132 kilometers (82 miles), this aqueduct historically supplied water to Carthage

item.dc:creator: StandingOutsideABrokenPhoneBoothWithMoneyInMyHand

item.title: It ain’t my day, week, month or year.
item.description: I want this to die in usersub. I just want to scream into the void. I’ve helped her so much in her life. I’ve helped her deal with her abusive dad, I’ve helped raise her child, I’ve taken out loans for her, helped her with everything I can possibly help with. I’m ruined emotionally, financially and mentally. I’m so tired of trying to be there for her. I’m tired of trying to be positive and cheerful all the time. I’m tired of acting like nothings wrong. I’m tired of acting like I love her. I am tired with how incompatible we are. I am tired of trying to make ends meet for us to stay together. I am tired of trying to be the father her child needs. I am tired of feeling like the bad guy whenever I disagree with something she did or said. I am tired of trying to be someone for her when she doesn’t even want to be someone for herself or her son. I am tired of being her emotional punching bag. I am tired of being treated this way. I am exhausted. I can’t do it anymore. I am broken.
item.dc:creator: Mal1t1a

item.title: I think I found my wedding dress!
item.description: This dress is so different than anything I thought I’d want. I’d always pictured myself wearing a black gown, so when I tried this on it was such a shock (in a good way). I felt like a pretty Victorian ghost and I loved it so much. I’m gonna go back in a few weeks with my aunt to show her and officially say yes!
item.dc:creator: bunniebby

item.title: No disputing that…
item.description: Disputing means to disagree with. Disagree means to not agree with. Please stop putting crayons in the fire alarm.
item.dc:creator: malbec

item.title: What's inside a Zeppelin?
item.description:
This is a post about Zeppelins, and specifically about their interiors, because I am always fascinated with special spaces like this. There will be a lot of pictures of the interiors of the Hindenburg and Graf zeppelins. There will not be a picture of the Hindenburg disaster, because that feels a little gruesome when I just want to geek out about the luxurious airship interiors. In the spirit of the internet, I will include some misinformation as well. See if you can spot it.
The crew and passenger areas of the Hindenburg were inside the balloon envelope, with only the control car and engines outside.
The Hindenburg on the ground. You can really get a sense of its scale in this picture.
Size comparison: Hindenburg vs. modern commercial jets. Heavier than air flight is for the birds.
Elevation showing the placement and scale of the passenger compartment in the Hindenburg.
Cutaway diagram showing passenger cabins and common areas.
Hindenburg dining area.
Another angle of the dining room.
Dining room with observation promenade along the side.
Meal time!
This is the only image I found with the tables arranged in banquet layout.
Coffee service in the promenade area.
Enjoying drinks in the bar area. Schnapps? Do Germans drink Schnapps with a cherry? Once my buddy and I got loaded on homemade Kirschwasser, but the cherries were all long gone.
The Hindenburg bar.
The Hindenburg's Smoking Room.
No open flame was allowed on the Hindenburg, so cigarettes and pipes had to be lit with electric lighters.
I also read that the smoking room was overpressurized so that no hydrogen could seep in from the surrounding gas chambers. It's difficult for me to imagine how that might work, what with the door and people coming and going and all. But maybe.
A passenger cabin on the Hindenburg. From what I can see, the Graf cabins were more luxurious. Still, I bet you slept like a baby in those beds as you float over the world.
Passenger cabin layout on the Hindenburg. The number of passengers seemed to vary widely. For a flight to Brazil it carried 50 passengers and 56 crew. After removing the aluminum grand piano and making some other changes, it could accommodate up to 70 passengers. On its final flight to New York it carried 36 passengers and 61 crew.
A passenger ticket from the Hindenburg's final flight. The ticket cost 1000 Reichsmark in 1937, and the Smithsonian says that was equivalent to $450 USD at the time. Adjusted for inflation that's $9483 USD in 2023. That's less than half Etihad's Apartment Class in modern times. Sadly, this passenger did not survive the crash.
The aluminum grand piano is in the background of this picture.
Crew quarters in the Hindenburg. I wish there were more pictures of crew accommodation, because to me these are some of the most interesting spaces. What was it like to live and work on an airship?
The crew dining room. Apologies for the portrait of the dickhead, but those were the times. This dining space reminds me of the dining areas in submarines.
The chef, hard at work over his electric stove. A modern airliner or ship would have rails to keep things from sliding around in turbulence, but zeppelins were so steady that wasn't necessary.
Looks like soup again.
Inside the control gondola. Behold the ship's officers. Very similar to a ship's bridge, right down to the speaking tube.
View from the control gondola of the inside the Hindenburg's hanger. Considering the size of the zeppelin, think of the size of its hanger.
By law, ship's instruments of the time were required to have robot faces.
A Hindenburg officer struggles against the ship's robot.
The navigator.
This is from the Graf Zeppelin. Same folding stool as the Hindenburg, but everything else is a little swankier. Although you could make the argument that the Graf's interior design is consistent with early 20th Century German decorative style, while the Hindenburg uses a more contemporary Internationalist style.
The Graf's passengers were suspended below the zeppelin's envelope, and so the passenger cabins had windows.
A crew member making beds.
The Graf's central corridor.
Inside the women's washroom.
The Graf's navigation office. We should all be so lucky to have an office with a view like this.
Layout of the Graf passenger areas.
This photo shows one of two successful attempts at mooring an airship to the Empire State Building. The building created updrafts that made it almost impossible to maneuver an airship close enough to dock, and it was out of the question to ask passengers to embark and disembark on a shaky ladder (as one site described it; prolly it was more like a steep staircase). The only other airship to dock up there simply made a newspaper delivery and left.
Additional cutaway diagram number 1.
Additional cutaway diagram number 2. Anyhoo, I think that's all I got to say about it at the moment. I hope you enjoyed it, and are as excited and hopeful as I am for the end of heavier-than-air flight and its barbarism. ******FP Edit******** Thank you guys for the upvotes. It’s nice to know I’m not the only one interested in stuff like this. Sometimes it’s a little lonely being me and the kind words and interest make it a lot less lonely. Have a great evening! *************SAUCES************ I swiped these images and some of the information from the following: https://www.airships.net/ (seriously, check this site out. the information is amazing and they talk about a lot more than just the German zeppelins) https://www.smithsonianmag.com/smithsonian-institution/a-hindenburg-passenger-ticket-possibly-the-only-one-to-still-exist-goes-on-view-51773461/ https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/LZ_129_Hindenburg https://www.openculture.com/2015/11/the-interior-of-the-hindenburg-revealed-in-1930s-color-photos.html https://www.boredpanda.com/inside-hindenburg-zeppelin-luxury-interior-vintage-photographs/ After I already made the rest of the post, I ran across this site which has some additional, very high quality airship photos: https://kmalexander.com/2017/03/21/how-passenger-airships-worked/
item.dc:creator: brotrustme

item.title: In memory of Kay Jay
item.description: In honor of Kay Jay's birthday, here are some of my favorite comics we made together. I'm so glad we collaborated when we had the chance. To everyone reading this: do something creative with someone you love and don't take the time you have together for granted.

item.title: There are two of them...
item.description: Of course, congratulations are in order for the expectant mother. Immense respect for competing on the world stage at seven months pregnant. But it's a 2-on-1 fight...

item.title: Doves getting cooked at '88 Olympics caused them to stop releasing them at opening
item.dc:creator: MangoWoodDesk

item.title: I will see you all again soon.
item.description: Years ago I came to Imgur looking for a few laughs when I was struggling with my mental health. I never expected to find such a wonderful and supportive community here. You guys have helped me through depression, alcoholism (4 years sober ?) endless drama with my family, and plenty of stupid decisions I've made over the years. Lately though the world has started to seem like a dark place to me and I find myself becoming more cynical and pessimistic. That is not the kind of person I believe myself to be and so I am taking some time off of social media to work on myself. I want to thank you all for your support and I wish you all the best. I will see you again soon.
item.dc:creator: Neale2No1
